title: Push-ups
about: Push-ups are a classic bodyweight exercise that bu... shoulders, and triceps while engaging the core for stability.
how_to_do: Start in a plank position with hands under shoulders
Lower your chest toward the ground, keeping elbows at 45degree
Push back up to the starting position
injury: Keep core engaged and spine neutral
Do not let hips sag or pike up
Avoid flaring elbows out too wide

title: Squats
about: Squats are a fundamental lower-body exercise that strengthen the legs and core. They primarily target the quadriceps, hamstrings, glutes, and calves.
how_to_do: Stand with feet hip-width apart
Hinge at hips and bend knees to lower into a squat
Keep chest up and back straight
Return to standing by driving through your heels
injury: Keep weight on heels and midfoot
Do not let knees cave inward
Maintain a neutral spine

title: Plank
about: The plank is an isometric core exercise that builds abdominal and back strength. It requires holding a straight-body position while engaging the abdominals, glutes, and shoulders.
how_to_do: Start in a forearm plank position (elbows under shoulders)
Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels
Hold the position while keeping abdominals and glutes tight
injury: Keep hips level (avoid sagging or piking)
Maintain a neutral neck (Do not look up or down)
Engage core to protect the lower back

title: Burpees
about: Burpees are a high-intensity full-body exercise that combines a squat, push-up, and jump. They elevate the heart rate and work the legs, chest, arms, and core for a powerful conditioning workout.
how_to_do: Squat down and place hands on the floor
Kick feet back into a plank and do a push-up
Jump feet forward, then explode up into a jump at the top
injury: Land softly to reduce impact
Keep core engaged during movement
Avoid lowering hips too far behind knees

title: Deadlift
about: Deadlifts are a compound weightlifting exercise that build the posterior chain. They work the hamstrings, glutes, lower back, and core, while also engaging the arms and shoulders.
how_to_do: Stand with feet hip-width apart and weights on floor
Hinge at hips and grasp the weight with straight arms
Drive through the heels to stand up, keeping back flat
injury: Keep spine neutral (avoid rounding back)
Engage core and glutes for stability
Lift with control (Do not jerk the weight)

title: Lunges
about: Lunges are a unilateral lower-body exercise that primarily target the quadriceps, glutes, and hamstrings, while also engaging the core for stability.
how_to_do: Step forward with one foot
Lower hips until both knees are bent at 90degree
Keep your front knee behind your toes
Push through your front heel to return to start
injury: Keep front knee aligned over ankle
Avoid leaning forward or letting knee go past toes
Maintain a neutral spine to protect your lower back

title: Jumping Jacks
about: Jumping jacks are a plyometric exercise that increases cardiovascular fitness and engages muscles in the legs, core, and shoulders.
how_to_do: Stand with feet together
Jump to spread legs and raise arms overhead
Land softly on balls of feet
Return to starting position and repeat
injury: Land softly with knees slightly bent
Keep knees aligned with toes
Maintain a steady rhythm

title: Mountain Climbers
about: Mountain climbers are a high-intensity, core-focused exercise performed from a plank position. They target the abdominals, shoulders, and hip flexors while also providing cardiovascular conditioning.
how_to_do: Start in a high plank position
Drive one knee toward your chest, then quickly alternate legs
Keep hips low and core engaged
Maintain a steady pace
injury: Keep hips low (avoid raising them high)
Engage core to protect your lower back
Land softly to avoid joint strain

title: High Knees
about: High knees are a plyometric running-in-place exercise that elevates heart rate. They engage the quadriceps, hamstrings, calves, glutes, and core while providing cardiovascular and calorie-burning benefits.
how_to_do: Stand tall with core engaged
Drive one knee up to hip level and switch legs rapidly
Pump arms in opposition to your legs
Maintain an upright posture
injury: Land lightly on the balls of your feet
Keep back straight and shoulders relaxed
Avoid leaning forward excessively

title: Leg Raises
about: Leg raises are a core-strengthening exercise performed lying on your back. They primarily target the lower abdominal muscles and hip flexors while also engaging the lower back.
benefits: Strengthens lower abs and hip flexors
how_to_do: Lie on your back with legs straight up or lowered
Keep your lower back pressed to the floor
Slowly lower legs toward the floor
Lift legs back to starting position
injury: Avoid arching your lower back
Move legs slowly with control
Do not swing or use momentum

title: Russian Twist
about: The Russian twist is a core exercise that builds strength in the abdominals and obliques while also engaging the shoulders for stability.
how_to_do: Sit on the floor with knees bent and feet lifted
Lean back slightly and hold arms together
Rotate torso to one side, then the other
Keep movements controlled
injury: Keep your spine straight and avoid slouching
Engage abs to control the movement
Breathe steadily

title: Bicycle Crunch
about: Bicycle crunches are a dynamic core exercise combining abdominal contraction with alternating leg motion. They target the rectus abdominis, transverse abdominis, obliques, and hip flexors.
benefits: Engages hip flexors for a full core workout
how_to_do: Lie on your back with legs lifted in tabletop position
Place hands behind your head
Lift shoulders and rotate to bring opposite elbow to knee
Alternate sides in a pedaling motion
injury: Keep your lower back pressed into the floor
Avoid pulling on your neck with your hands
Perform movements with control

title: Tricep Dips
about: Tricep dips are a bodyweight exercise that target the triceps on the back of the arms while also engaging the chest and shoulders.
benefits: Strengthens the triceps and upper arms
how_to_do: Place hands on parallel bars or edge of bench behind you
Lower your body by bending elbows to 90degree
Keep your body straight and elbows close to the sides
Press back up to the starting position
injury: Avoid dipping too low (stop when elbows at or above shoulder level)
Keep shoulders down and back
Don’t lock out elbows too forcefully

title: Bicep Curls
about: Bicep curls are an upper-arm exercise that isolate the biceps muscles. They build strength in the front of the arms and forearms.
how_to_do: Stand with dumbbells at your sides
Curl the weights by bending elbows, keeping them close to your torso
Raise weights until forearms are vertical
Lower with control
injury: Keep your back straight and core tight
Avoid swinging the weights using momentum
Keep elbows stationary and close to your body

title: Shoulder Press
about: The shoulder press is a compound upper-body exercise that primarily targets the deltoid muscles in the shoulders. It also engages the triceps, upper chest, and core for stability.
how_to_do: Stand or sit with weights at shoulder height
Press the weights overhead until arms are fully extended
Lower the weights back down to shoulders with control
injury: Keep back neutral and core engaged
Avoid shrugging shoulders or flaring elbows
Perform the press in a controlled manner

title: Bench Press
about: The bench press is a compound chest exercise performed lying on a bench. It primarily targets the pectoral muscles, with secondary emphasis on the shoulders and triceps.
benefits: Builds chest, shoulder, and triceps strength
how_to_do: Lie on a bench with feet on the floor
Grip the barbell or dumbbells with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width
Lower the weight to your mid-chest
Push the weight back up until arms are fully extended
injury: Keep feet flat on the floor and lower back slightly arched
Do not bounce the weight off your chest
Avoid locking elbows aggressively

title: Pull-Ups
about: Pull-ups are a bodyweight exercise that primarily strengthen the upper back (latissimus dorsi) while also working the arms (biceps and forearms) and shoulders.
how_to_do: Grab an overhead bar with a shoulder-width grip, palms facing away
Hang with arms fully extended
Pull your body upward until your chin clears the bar
Lower yourself back down with control
injury: Avoid swinging or kipping excessively
Lower down slowly to protect elbows
Engage core and shoulders for stability

title: Lat Pulldown
about: The cable lat pulldown is a compound back exercise that primarily targets the latissimus dorsi (lats) while also engaging the biceps, shoulders, and other upper-back muscles.
how_to_do: Sit and grasp the overhead bar with a wide grip
Lean back slightly and keep chest up
Pull the bar down towards your upper chest, focusing on squeezing your lats
Slowly return the bar to the starting position
injury: Avoid leaning back too far
Keep your spine neutral (no arch)
Use controlled motion (no jerking)

title: Glute Bridge
about: Glute bridges are a hip-extension exercise that strengthen the glutes and hamstrings while also engaging the core.
how_to_do: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on floor
Push through your heels to lift your hips off the ground
Squeeze your glutes at the top of the movement
Lower hips back down with control
injury: Keep your core engaged to avoid over-arching lower back
Do not lift hips higher than necessary
Move slowly to maintain control

title: Cycling (Stationary Bike)
about: Cycling on a stationary bike is a low-impact cardiovascular exercise that improves heart and lung health while strengthening the leg muscles (quadriceps, hamstrings, calves, glutes).
benefits: Burns calories and supports weight loss
how_to_do: Adjust the seat height and strap your feet to the pedals
Pedal at a steady pace, adjusting resistance as needed
Keep an upright posture with core engaged
Vary intensity or intervals for a challenge
injury: Adjust seat to avoid knee strain (knees slightly bent)
Maintain a neutral spine posture
Do not pedal with toes pointed downward (use flat foot)
